operating_state: Total sales and revenues increased 11% to $14.247 billion in Q2 2022 and $27.8 billion for the six-month period, driven by favorable price realization and higher services volume across all primary segments. Order backlog reached approximately $28.5 billion at quarter-end, up $2.0 billion from Q1, primarily driven by Energy & Transportation demand.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin compressed to 13.6% from 14.5% in the prior year period despite revenue growth, as favorable price realization was offset by higher material and freight costs. Short-term incentive compensation expense increased substantially with full-year 2022 expected at $1.6 billion compared to $1.3 billion in 2021.
material_events: The Board approved an 8% quarterly dividend increase to $1.20 per share in June 2022 and authorized a new $15 billion share repurchase program effective August 1, 2022. The company repurchased $1.92 billion of common stock during the first six months with capital expenditures increasing to $594 million from $430 million. Restructuring costs are expected at approximately $600 million for full-year 2022 while delivering incremental operating cost benefits.
macro_exposures: The company continues monitoring COVID-19 impacts, supply chain disruptions, and inflationary pressures affecting components, transportation and raw materials. Transportation shortages and supplier availability issues have resulted in delays and increased costs pressuring production facilities. Supply chain finance programs processed $2.6 billion in settlements during the first six months compared to $1.9 billion prior year.
forward_outlook: Price realization is expected to more than offset manufacturing cost increases for full-year 2022, with capital expenditures projected at approximately $1.5 billion. The company maintains its long-term cash deployment strategy focused on mid-A rating maintenance and returning substantially all free cash flow to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases.
uncertainty_profile: Conditions remain dynamic requiring ongoing monitoring and adjustment of contingency plans to address supply chain challenges and meet increasing customer demand. The company faces potential increased borrowing costs if credit ratings are downgraded and continues taking appropriate price actions in response to rising costs.
